sectionsUnavailable: No direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample report has been captured.

reportOverview.plainEnglishMeaning: This run can describe supplied lactose-related marker context and reproduce sibling nutrition sample output structure, but it cannot diagnose metabolic disease, infer diabetes or insulin resistance, predict glucose, interpret labs, set nutrient targets, or recommend diet or supplements.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, metabolic inference, or treatment recommendation.
resultRows.description: Local synthetic fixture row for conservative lactose-related marker context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, environment, medications, ancestry, and professional review matter for nutrition and metabolic-health decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated metabolic, glucose, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.description: Local synthetic fixture row marking the package-specific calibrated model and non-genetic context unavailable.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not make diet, supplement, nutrient, metabolic, glucose, symptom, lab, medication, diagnosis, or treatment conclusions without validated evidence.

resultRows.item: Alcohol, caffeine, lactose, and gluten summary cards
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary cards show Alcohol: Slow Metabolism; Caffeine: Fast Metabolism; Lactose: Likely Sensitive; Gluten: Not Likely Sensitive, with a legend separating likely-sensitive from not-likely-sensitive labels.
resultRows.description: The sample summary page presents the four result cards before detailed substance and sensitivity sections.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample introduces alcohol and caffeine as substances whose metabolism can vary with genetic markers, then notes that slower metabolism markers may mean the body takes longer to clear alcohol or caffeine.
resultRows.description: The substances section precedes the detailed Alcohol Metabolism and Caffeine Metabolism pages.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.item: ADH1C gene table and slow-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Alcohol Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have slow alcohol metabolism. The visible gene table lists ADH1C marker GP000071 genotype TT with slow alcohol metabolism impact, plus ADH1C GP000072 genotype CC and ADH1C GP000073 genotype TT with normal alcohol metabolism impact.
resultRows.description: The page also lists common alcohol sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking drinks, spacing them out, and drinking water.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.item: CYP1A2 gene table and fast-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Caffeine Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have fast caffeine metabolism. The visible gene table lists CYP1A2 marker GP000071 genotype TT with fast caffeine metabolism impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common caffeine sources such as coffee, tea, soft drinks, energy drinks, chocolate, certain medications, and yerba mate, plus a sleep-timing action block.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.item: Food sensitivity versus food allergy explanation
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Sensitivities section distinguishes food sensitivity from food allergy, explains that sensitivities can involve difficulty digesting components such as lactose or gluten, and names non-genetic contributors such as gut microbiome imbalance, gastrointestinal disease, stress, age, additives, sleep, and physical inactivity.
resultRows.description: The section explicitly says genetics can play a role but do not work in isolation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.item: HLA-DQA1 and HLA-DQ8 gene table with not-likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Gluten Sensitivity page says the sample genome is not likely sensitive to gluten. The visible gene table lists HLA-DQA1 marker GP000071 genotype TT and HLA-DQ8 marker GP000072 genotype CC, both with not-likely-sensitive-to-gluten impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common gluten sources and includes a what-can-you-do block about a temporary gluten-removal trial.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.item: LCT and MCM6-LCT gene table with likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Lactose Sensitivity page says the sample genome is more likely to be sensitive to lactose. The visible gene table lists LCT marker GP000071 genotype TT with more-likely-sensitive-to-lactose impact and MCM6-LCT marker GP000072 genotype CC with less-likely-sensitive-to-lactose impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common lactose sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking dairy reactions, considering lower-lactose foods, and discussing dairy reduction or lactose enzymes with a healthcare practitioner.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only for Nutritional & Metabolic Health formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes or insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, lab interpretation, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

metabolicContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a metabolic diagnosis or treatment guide.
Bring symptoms, diet history, labs, medications, supplements, and health goals to a clinician or registered dietitian for nutrition and metabolic-health decisions.
Do not change foods, supplements, medications, or treatment based on genotype alone or sibling sample rows.

missingInputs: No direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample report has been captured.
Sibling Food Sensitivity and Healthy Nutrition sample rows are used only as observed output-structure evidence.
No calibrated metabolic, glucose, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
Broader diet, symptoms, labs, medications, medical history, ancestry applicability, environment, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition, food response, nutrient status, symptoms, glucose, labs, medications, and metabolic outcomes depend on genetics plus clinical and lifestyle context and professional review.
Sibling sample rows show output structure only and should not be treated as local findings or a direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health mock report.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose metabolic disease, infer diabetes or insulin resistance, predict glucose, interpret labs, set nutrient targets, or prescribe diet or supplement changes.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only and does not include raw genome data.

The report is educational and does not diagnose metabolic disease, diagnose diabetes, infer insulin resistance, predict glucose, interpret labs, prescribe a diet, recommend supplements, set nutrient targets, change medications, or provide treatment guidance.
Direct Nutritional & Metabolic Health sample-report rows and exact detail-page parity are still unavailable.
